Risk Desc:
Context:
Azure DevOps (ADO) is used as the project dashboard where all features, user stories, tasks, and pull requests (PRs) are tracked. As per the defined process, PRs must be linked at the User Story level to ensure proper traceability and reporting.

Condition:
The team did not consistently maintain the required linking structure.

PRs were linked to tasks instead of user stories.

Proper links were also missing at the feature and user story levels.

Consequence:

Reduced traceability between code changes and user stories.

Incomplete updates visible in the ADO dashboard.

Gaps in reporting shared with the client.

Potential impact on client communication and relationship due to missing or unclear status updates. Project-Level Project Management                          Planning      Moderate    Unlikely    Medium
Category:
Created a list of Do’s and Don’ts for Azure DevOps (ADO) to ensure proper usage of the dashboard.

Conducted a knowledge-sharing session with the team to educate them on correct ADO practices, including proper linking of features, user stories, tasks, and pull requests (PRs).

Highlighted the importance of linking PRs at the User Story level instead of tasks to maintain proper traceability.

Defined and shared action items for improving ADO hygiene, including regular checks and adherence to the linking standards.
